channel.title: #DigitalBlackOutIran
channel.description: Daily simulation of how much GDP the world and the top 10 economies would lose if their own internet were blacked out for as many days as Iran's.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 119
item.description: 2026-06-26 blackout simulation archive: United States, China and Germany top the loss table; world scenario totals $81.26B/day.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 118
item.description: Day 118 blackout simulation report: United States $20.13B/day, China $12.76B/day, world $81.26B/day.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 117
item.description: SEO update for 2026-06-24: simulated blackout losses show United States at $20.13B/day and world exposure at $81.26B/day.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 116
item.description: Internet blackout day 116: modeled daily loss reaches $81.26B worldwide, with United States and China leading the country ranking.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 115
item.description: 2026-06-22 update: simulated internet blackout losses put United States first, China second, with world exposure at $81.26B/day.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 114
item.description: Day 114 economic impact simulation: if internet went dark locally, United States would lose $20.13B/day; world $81.26B/day.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 113
item.description: 2026-06-20 internet blackout simulation: day 113, world scenario $81.26B/day. United States leads at $20.13B/day.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 112
item.description: Internet outage simulation day 112: world daily loss $81.26B, cumulative world loss $6.67T; United States remains the largest economy case.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 111
item.description: 2026-06-18 blackout simulation archive: United States, China and Germany top the loss table; world scenario totals $81.00B/day.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 110
item.description: Day 110 blackout simulation report: United States $20.13B/day, China $12.66B/day, world $80.65B/day.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 109
item.description: SEO update for 2026-06-16: simulated blackout losses show United States at $20.13B/day and world exposure at $80.30B/day.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 108
item.description: Internet blackout day 108: modeled daily loss reaches $79.95B worldwide, with United States and China leading the country ranking.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 107
item.description: 2026-06-14 update: simulated internet blackout losses put United States first, China second, with world exposure at $79.60B/day.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 106
item.description: Day 106 economic impact simulation: if internet went dark locally, United States would lose $20.13B/day; world $79.24B/day.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 105
item.description: 2026-06-12 internet blackout simulation: day 105, world scenario $78.89B/day. United States leads at $20.13B/day.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 104
item.description: Internet outage simulation day 104: world daily loss $78.54B, cumulative world loss $6.03T; United States remains the largest economy case.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 103
item.description: 2026-06-10 blackout simulation archive: United States, China and Germany top the loss table; world scenario totals $78.18B/day.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 102
item.description: Day 102 blackout simulation report: United States $20.13B/day, China $12.22B/day, world $77.83B/day.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 101
item.description: SEO update for 2026-06-08: simulated blackout losses show United States at $20.13B/day and world exposure at $77.48B/day.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 100
item.description: Internet blackout day 100: modeled daily loss reaches $77.12B worldwide, with United States and China leading the country ranking.
